message: Node started: Analyze the cost function C = sum((tx - fx) + (ty - fy)). Note that this is equivalent to the sum of Manhattan distances of the edges in the construction tree. Since we start at (0,0) and must reach all (ai, bi), the minimum possible cost C is achieved by finding a Minimum Spanning Tree or a Steiner Tree structure that respects the monotonicity constraint (tx >= fx and ty >= fy). Propose a greedy or MST-based strategy that ensures every target is reachable from (0,0) while minimizing the sum of coordinate differences. Consider sorting targets by x then y to simplify the construction.
